back_translation: When He came down from the mountain, great crowds followed Him.
Behold, a leper came and bowed down before Him, saying: 'Lord, if You are willing, You are able to make me clean.'
He stretched out His hand and touched him, saying: 'I am willing. Be clean.' Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
Then Jesus said to him: 'Be careful, tell no one. But go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the sacrifice that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
When He entered the city of Capernaum, a centurion came and pleaded with Him,
saying: 'Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, suffering terribly.'
He said to him: 'I will go and heal him.'
The centurion answered: 'Lord, I am not worthy to have You come under the roof of my house; but just speak a single word, and my servant will recover.
For I myself am also under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, 'Go,' and he goes; to another, 'Come,' and he comes; and to my slave, 'Do this,' and he does it.'
When Jesus heard this, He was amazed, and said to those who were following Him: 'I tell you the truth: I have not found anyone in Israel with such great faith.
I tell you: many will come from the east and the west, and will recline at table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
But the children of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness; there there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'
Then Jesus said to the centurion: 'Go; let it be done as you have believed.' And in that very hour, his servant was healed.
When Jesus entered into Peter's house, He saw Peter's mother-in-law lying sick with fever.
He touched her hand, and the fever left her; then she got up and served Him.
When evening came, people brought to Him many who were demon-possessed. He cast out those spirits with a single word, and healed all the sick.
This was to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet: 'He bore our sicknesses and carried away our diseases.'
When Jesus saw the great crowd around Him, He gave orders to cross to the other side.
A scribe came and said to Him: 'Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.'
Jesus said to him: 'Foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.'
Another disciple said to Him: 'Lord, allow me to go bury my father first.'
But Jesus said to him: 'Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.'
When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.
Behold, a great storm arose on the sea, so that the waves swept over the boat. But He was sleeping.
The disciples came and woke Him, saying: 'Lord, save us! We are about to perish!'
He said to them: 'You of little faith, why are you so cowardly?' Then He got up, rebuked the wind and the sea, and the sea became completely calm.
Those people were amazed and said: 'Who is this man, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?'
When He had crossed to the other side, into the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men came out from the burial tombs to meet Him. Both were so violent that no one could pass that way.
Behold, they cried out: 'Son of God, what is between us and You? Have You come here to torment us before the appointed time?'
In a place far from them, a great herd of pigs was feeding.
The demons pleaded with Him: 'If You are going to cast us out, send us into that herd of pigs.'
He said to them: 'Go!' They came out and entered the herd of pigs, and behold, the whole herd ran and plunged down from the cliff into the sea, and drowned to death in that sea.
The pig herders fled into the city and told everything, including the story of the demon-possessed men.
Behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw Him, they pleaded with Him to depart from their region.
